patch.value: GH-590: cycle-creating blocked_by mutations succeed silently — items deadlock out of pm next with no inline feedback

GitHub issue #590: a dependency mutation that closes a blocked_by cycle exits 0 with no warning; both items permanently leave pm next ready/recommended and only pm graph audit (ordering_cycle) or pm deps (cycle:true) explain why — nothing points there. Parent-cycle mutations already fail fast (precedent); ordering-kind dep mutations must warn inline naming the cycle path (or reject with an override flag), consistent with that precedent.

Mutation that would create an ordering-kind dependency cycle warns inline with the cycle path and pm graph audit pointer, or rejects with an explicit override flag; parent-cycle fail-fast precedent cited in contract docs; existing stored legacy cycles unaffected; regression coverage for update/update-many/create --dep paths
